moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; higher hitstun than other light normals allows a natural combo into 214LK/214MK; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: Moves forward during startup; easily hitconfirmable into TC follow-up (always a true blockstring even if delayed); high active frame count makes it good for oki; Punish Counter causes the follow-up Target Combo (5MP~MK) to change properties (causes hard knockdown instead of standing reset); strong Drive Rush check that can combo into 236LP on counter-hit for a medal

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Very lenient hitconfirm window (always a true blockstring even if delayed); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes the follow-up Target Combo (5HP~HP) to change properties (causes limited juggle state knockdown instead of standing reset)

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: Longest ranged light normal (important for punishing -5 or -6 attacks); does not naturally combo into 214LK/214MK outside of Punish Counter situations

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: Front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 9-12, making it a good poke against low attacks; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: 3f extra recovery on block/whiff; incredibly long range poke that can combo into 214LK/214MK on Punish Counter from max range; Punish Counter puts airborne opponents into spinning free juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; the slow startup and recovery makes it especially vulnerable to Drive Impact

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; true blockstring when chained into 5LP/2LP; cannot combo into 214LK/214MK, but gives more reliable 3-hit light confirm; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: 4f extra recovery on whiff; Manon's primary combo starter

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Very strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; the hurtbox on Manon's arm and head is invincible to airborne strikes, and her hurtbox remains in a crouching state for the entire animation allowing for late anti-airs to work

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; primary low combo starter; less pushback than other chainable light normals (allows tick throws and easier 3-light string confirms)

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Good range low poke that leads to combos and frame advantage when used out of Drive Rush; high pushback on hit and block; after Drive Rush, can link into 5MP at closer ranges

moves.name: Croisee
moves.notes: Moves forward during startup, giving it more range but also making it easily punished on block; has juggle potential

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; excellent horizontal air-to-air hitbox; lower body projectile invuln 8-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead (requires perfect safe jump timing)

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: Incredible hitbox priority and downward reach (best option for most jump-ins); lower body projectile invuln 11-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Back MK
moves.notes: Long TC cancel window into an overhead, but will not combo on hit if delayed too long; good anti-air hitbox (cannot hit cross-ups); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state (follow-up TC can continue juggle, or can juggle 236KK/SA1/SA2 on a high anti-air)

moves.name: Révérence
moves.notes: Strong punish starter and combo filler after Drive Rush routes; high active frame count makes it very strong as a meaty; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; lenient hitconfirm window, but late cancels into 236P, 214HK, or 214KK can fail to combo

moves.name: Tomoe Derriére
moves.notes: Not a low attack despite the animation; works as an anti-air to to invincibility on kicking leg; puts opponents into limited juggle state; can hit cross-up; kicks airborne opponents backwards for a side switch; Punish Counter also switches sides and gives a Hard Knockdown, allowing follow-up oki; only the 1st active frame can hit grounded opponents (active frames 2-9 hit airborne)

moves.name: À Terre
moves.notes: Pulls opponent close to Manon for a strike/throw mixup on hit (but also easily punishable on block); always a true blockstring from 5MP; after Punish Counter 5MP, becomes a hard knockdown (HKD +32) that sets up strong oki anywhere on the screen

moves.name: En Haut
moves.notes: Forces crouch on hit; 2f blockstring gap when canceled from 4MK, which can be increased by delaying the cancel for different frame trap timings; combos naturally from 4MK unless the TC cancel is delayed; has juggle potential and puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG state (useful after a 4MK anti-air); special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

moves.name: Temps Lié
moves.notes: Pulls opponent close to Manon for a strike/throw mixup on hit, but also easily punishable on block; 2f extra recovery on block; always a true blockstring even when delayed; easily hitconfirmed from 5HP; after Punish Counter 5HP, becomes a knockdown (KD +48 limited juggle state) that can combo into 236KK or SA2; this Punish version has extended range and inflicts damage scaling on the follow-up juggle

moves.name: Allongé
moves.notes: Causes spike knockdown after 2HP hits an airborne opponent; slightly better KD advantage when opponent is juggled high in the air

moves.name: Ouchi Gari
moves.notes: Can manually time a throw loop in the corner; Drive Rush also allows an auto-timed meaty throw that can be mixed up with 2MP or 4HP; a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Uchi Mata
moves.notes: Side switches; after corner side switch, can walk for a pseudo throw loop or Drive Rush for a manually timed strike/throw mixup; can get true throw oki vs. wide characters (1f timing vs. Marisa, 2f timing vs. Blanka/E.Honda/Zangief); midscreen, can chase down with DR~5HP but no real strike/throw mixup; a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Glissade
moves.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

moves.name: Écarté (Block)
moves.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze; see Drive Reversal.

moves.name: Drive Parry
moves.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

moves.name: Parry Drive Rush
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.506 (min/throw), 1.417 (min/block), 2.347 (max/final DR frame)

moves.name: Drive Rush Cancel
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

moves.name: LP Manège Doré
moves.notes: Increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2000/2300/2700/3000/3700; Super Gain at Medal Lv. 1-5: 3000/3000/4000/4000/5000; cannot be special canceled into; slowest startup with long range

moves.name: MP Manège Doré
moves.notes: Increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2000/2300/2700/3000/3700; Super Gain at Medal Lv. 1-5: 3000/3000/4000/4000/5000; cannot be special canceled into; medium startup with medium range

moves.name: HP Manège Doré
moves.notes: Increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2000/2300/2700/3000/3700; Super Gain at Medal Lv. 1-5: 3000/3000/4000/4000/5000; cannot be special canceled into; fastest startup with shortest range

moves.name: OD Manège Doré
moves.notes: Increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2000/2300/2700/3000/3700; Super Gain at Medal Lv. 1-5: 3000/3000/4000/4000/5000; cannot be special canceled into; medium startup with long range

moves.name: LK Rond-point
moves.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; SA3 cancel occurs on 2nd hit (allows it to combo vs. grounded opponents); the 1st hit can whiff when canceled into at max range (SA3 cancel at this range usually whiffs and has a much shorter hitconfirm window)

moves.name: MK Rond-point
moves.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; SA3 cancel is only useful if the attack is absorbed by armor; puts opponents into limited juggle state (very high anti-air is required for a follow-up juggle); the 1st hit tends to whiff when canceled into at max range

moves.name: HK Rond-point
moves.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; the 1st hit can combo into SA3 vs. grounded opponents (causes SA3 to incur 15% immediate damage scaling instead of the usual 5%); the final 2 recovery frames can be canceled into any special or super on hit (allows for better juggles); puts opponents into limited juggle state with a higher float state than other meterless versions (but still requires an airborne hit to combo into a follow-up)

moves.name: OD Rond-point
moves.notes: Strong anti-air with an enormous hitbox, but cannot hit cross-up; 2nd hit does 400 damage if 1st hit whiffs; the final 5 recovery frames can be canceled into any special or super on hit (allows for better juggles); puts opponents into limited juggle state (has consistent juggle follow-ups); 1st hit can combo into SA3 when canceled (causes SA3 to incur 15% immediate damage scaling instead of the usual 5%); 2nd hit juggles into SA2 when canceled (SA3 cancel only useful if attack is absorbed by armor); the 1st hit has extra range against grounded opponents

moves.name: LK Dégagé
moves.notes: Long range low attack that can catch opponents by surprise, but very unsafe if blocked; can be juggled into; leaves opponent close for follow-up oki even midscreen

moves.name: MK Dégagé
moves.notes: Long range tool for combos and punishes, leading to great corner carry

moves.name: HK Dégagé
moves.notes: Forces crouch on hit; 1st active frame can only hit airborne opponents (functionally 21f startup); works as a low crush but not actually airborne

moves.name: OD Dégagé
moves.notes: 1st active frame cannot hit crouching opponents (functionally 23f as an overhead); 1f better advantage on hit vs. crouching (allows links into 5MP/2MP for improved damage and oki); blockstun is reduced by 1f on active frames 2-3 (still -3 if stand blocked on frame 23)

moves.name: LP Renversé
moves.notes: Hitgrab that works in combos/juggles and can be blocked; total damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 1350/1500/1650/1800/1950; Super Gain on Hit at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2150/2150/3150/3150/4150; 1st hit Super Gain is only 150 self / 105 opponent regardless of Medal Level; increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; 1st hit restands juggled opponents and allows a cancel into SA3

moves.name: MP Renversé
moves.notes: Hitgrab that works in combos/juggles and can be blocked; total damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 1400/1550/1700/1850/2000; Super Gain on Hit at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2150/2150/3150/3150/4150; 1st hit Super Gain is only 150 self / 105 opponent regardless of Medal Level; increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; 1st hit restands juggled opponents and allows a cancel into SA3

moves.name: HP Renversé
moves.notes: Hitgrab that works in combos/juggles and can be blocked; total damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 1500/1650/1800/1950/2100; Super Gain on Hit at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2150/2150/3150/3150/4150; 1st hit Super Gain is only 150 self / 105 opponent regardless of Medal Level; increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; 1st hit restands juggled opponents and allows a cancel into SA3

moves.name: OD Renversé
moves.notes: Hitgrab that works in combos/juggles and can be blocked; total damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 1500/1650/1800/1950/2100; Super Gain on Hit at Medal Lv. 1-5: 2150/2150/3150/3150/4150; 1st hit Super Gain is only 150 self / 105 opponent regardless of Medal Level; increases Medal Level by 1 on hit; unlike the meterless versions, this one cannot be canceled into any Super; leaves Manon much closer to opponent in the corner on hit, allowing for better oki than other enders

moves.name: Renversé Feint
moves.notes: Travel distance for LP/MP/HP versions: 0.85/1.10/1.14

moves.name: Grand Fouetté
moves.notes: Startup of the initial spin depends on button strength; any invincibility from 236P is lost as soon as the kick follow-up starts (can slightly delay the follow-up to extend invincibility); cancel into SA3 is only useful if the kick is absorbed by armor; puts opponent into limited juggle state (free juggle state on Punish Counter); can be spaced to be safer in neutral

moves.name: OD Grand Fouetté
moves.notes: Any invincibility from 236PP is lost as soon as the kick follow-up starts (can slightly delay the follow-up to extend invincibility); puts opponent into free juggle state on hit; can be spaced to be safer in neutral; combos naturally from 4HP, making it an excellent combo tool for damage and corner carry

moves.name: Arabesque
moves.notes: Long range sliding low attack; switches sides with opponent on hit (except at max range); can be used in juggles; can trade vs. projectiles at certain ranges, allowing for a follow-up juggle

moves.name: Étoile
moves.notes: If blocked, the last hit whiffs on crouching opponents; can be juggled into; extended KD time for opponent if they fall out before 3rd hit; full damage distribution: 450,400,500x2,950

moves.name: Pas de Deux
moves.notes: Command throw that can be comboed into; opponent can jump on reaction after the Super freeze; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 4500/4600/4700/4900/5100; cannot hit opponents behind Manon; juggles into meterless 236P grant a restand that combos into SA3; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.4 Drive bars for Manon; Range: 1.463 (2nd active frame 1.489)
